methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Hexana is a seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Sylvain Nizou and Paul Gauthe that has raised €25M to date. The comparable universe consists of 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private nuclear energy companies, including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Applied Atomics.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable to this valuation. The nuclear energy sector contains only 4 same-sector peers overall, and filtering for stage alignment (seed or adjacent-stage) and valuation-tier comparability yielded fewer than 3 datapoints—the minimum required for robust statistical triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given Hexana's specialized technology risk profile and capital intensity relative to broader software or biotech seed cohorts.

Given the methodological constraint, this analysis cannot support a specific valuation range with confidence. The primary uncertainties are the absence of sufficient narrowly-comparable seed-stage nuclear peers and the heterogeneity of the private peer set across development phases and technology approaches. Further valuation work would require either (1) expansion to adjacent-stage peers with explicit stage-adjustment modeling, (2) discounted cash flow analysis tied to project-specific milestones, or (3) accumulation of additional seed-stage nuclear raises to establish statistical density. The €25M raised to date provides a floor reference, but alone does not establish fair value.
